data: No aliquot and keep at 4¢XC if arrive at PMH within 48 hours; otherwise aliquot and keep at -20¢XC
Aid in diagnosis of Infectious Mononucleosis (IM).

Limitation for Test:The result should be interpreted in light of the clinical symptoms shown by the patient. Occasionally detectable levels of heterophile antibodies are late in developing in patients symptomatic for infectious mononucleosis. If symptoms persist, it is recommended to repeat the test in several days. False positive and false-negative results have been reported.

Additional Information:About 10% of adult population with IM will not develop heterophile antibodies.  Failure to develop heterophile antibodies occurs even more frequently in children. In these instances the presence of Epstein-Barr virus antibodies may occur.
